_meta.dna: moat(product) = differentiation(real) x switching_cost(high) x network(growing) x speed(first)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 10-15 seed questions from an adversarial competitor perspective. During execution, expand to 100 questions per persona. Every competitor wants you to fail — zero credit for architecture alone, distribution is measured in users not papers.

name: Tower of Competitors
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(100q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM
aggregate_prior: Architecture A, Distribution F, PMF UNPROVEN (15% floor pass rate, 100% boss floors)

questions: We have 100+ AgentSkills. You have 81 recipes. Why would anyone choose fewer automations?
We ship faster than you. What is your release velocity in the last 90 days?
We have a community of 500+ contributors. You have one founder. Bus factor?
Our users can extend the system themselves. Your store is sealed. Why?
We run without any LLM dependency. Can your system operate without calling an external AI?
We don't require user approval for every action. Why does yours create this friction?
Our installation is one command. What is your actual install-to-first-task time?
We have documented RCE vulnerability mitigations. Have you audited your attack surface?
We support Docker, Helm, systemd. What deployment targets do you support?
We have 5,000 GitHub stars. What is your social proof metric?
Our recipes are community-sourced and grow daily. How do yours grow?
We integrate with 30+ external tools. How many integrations does your recipe library cover?
Our pricing is free (self-hosted). Justify your $8/month charge against a free alternative.
We have video tutorials for every major workflow. What learning resources do you provide?
Our codebase is fully open source. Yours is private. Why should an enterprise trust you?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full domain. Prioritize questions that expose fixable competitive gaps.

questions: We have 15,000+ GitHub stars and growing. What is your GitHub star count?
We are pure OSS with MIT license. You have a private core. Why would developers choose you?
We integrate with any LLM with 2 lines of code. How complex is your LLM setup?
We have zero governance overhead — no approval dialogs, no evidence chains. Users love speed.
Our Python SDK is pip-installable. What is your actual installation complexity?
We have 200+ contributors from the open source community. How many contributors do you have?
Our benchmark shows we complete tasks 40% faster without approval delays. How do you respond?
We support async execution out of the box. Does your system handle concurrent automation?
Our framework works with any browser profile. How does your session management compare?
We have examples for 50+ use cases in our repo. Where are yours?
Our community answers support questions in hours on GitHub Issues. What is your support SLA?
We are the de facto standard for Python browser automation. What standard are you building?
Our users can inspect every line of code we run. Can yours?
We have been cited in 50+ academic papers. What is your research credibility?
Our framework has no opinion on evidence or governance. Is this a feature or a bug?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full domain. Prioritize questions that expose fixable competitive gaps.

questions: We are built by the same company that made Claude. Why would anyone use a third-party wrapper?

Our API pricing is transparent. Is your 20% markup clearly disclosed?
We have a legal team and compliance framework for enterprise. Do you?
We are expanding our capabilities every month. How will you keep up with our velocity?
Our API is used by the world's largest companies. Who are your named enterprise customers?
We will eventually build native consent and evidence features. Why build Solace now?
Our documentation is written by Anthropic engineers. Who wrote yours?
Our uptime SLA is backed by Amazon Web Services infrastructure. What backs yours?
We support every Claude capability natively. Which capabilities are you missing?
Our enterprise contracts are handled by Anthropic's sales team. Who handles yours?

questions: We have 3 billion users pre-installed. What is your total addressable install base?

questions: We run on-device with no data leaving the user's machine. How does your local-first compare?

questions: We are a Chrome extension with zero installation required. Your product requires a terminal. Explain.

questions: 5.7 billion people use smartphones. Your product is desktop-only. Is this a permanent constraint?
Mobile-first automation represents 60%+ of daily digital activity. Why are you ignoring it?
When an iOS automation agent ships, every desktop-only tool becomes niche. Are you prepared?
Your local-first architecture requires a desktop. How does this survive a mobile-first future?
Shortcuts on iOS and Tasker on Android already automate mobile workflows. How do you compete?
WhatsApp, Instagram, TikTok, and WeChat are mobile-only primary platforms. How do your recipes run there?
When Apple Intelligence adds browser automation to iPhone Safari, what is your response?
Mobile users expect a native app experience. What is your native mobile strategy?
Mobile-first markets (Africa, Southeast Asia) represent billions of users. How do you serve them?
Your terminal-based installation is irrelevant on mobile. What is your mobile installation story?
Battery life, data usage, and background execution are mobile constraints. Have you addressed these?
Progressive Web App (PWA) technology could bridge your gap. Have you explored this?
Mobile push notifications could power your approval workflow. Do you support this?
What percentage of your target users spend more than 50% of their computing time on mobile?
Mobile biometric authentication (Face ID, fingerprint) could enhance your consent model. Any plans?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full domain. Prioritize questions that expose fixable competitive gaps.

questions: WhatsApp has 3 billion users. What is your messaging automation story?
Telegram bots have been automating workflows for years without needing a browser. What is your differentiator?
WeChat Mini Programs reach 1.2 billion users in China. How do your recipes reach them?
LINE, KakaoTalk, Zalo dominate Asian markets you claim to serve. Do your recipes run there?
SMS automation (Twilio) is more reliable than browser automation. Why browser-first?
WhatsApp Business API enables customer communication at scale. Do your recipes integrate with it?
Your security decision to exclude messaging excludes 3B+ users. Is this the right tradeoff?
When messaging platforms add native automation, your browser automation will be redundant. Prepare?
iMessage Business Messaging is coming to enterprise. What is your Apple messaging strategy?
Your evidence chain could be shared via messaging for approval. Is this designed?
RCS (Rich Communication Services) is becoming the SMS replacement. Does your recipe library support it?
Email automation is declining as messaging grows. How does your roadmap reflect this shift?
The approval workflow via messaging could be more natural than a desktop dialog. Have you explored this?
Businesses are moving customer service from email to messaging. How does your recipe library serve this?
Messaging automation is a security risk (WhatsApp phishing, Telegram scams). How do you distinguish your product?

questions: Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Cursor already own code automation. What gap do you fill?

When IDE agents add browser automation as a built-in feature, what happens to your product?

questions: Apple Vision Pro and Meta Quest represent a new computing paradigm. Is your product adaptable?
Spatial computing will require new interaction models beyond keyboard/mouse/browser. Are you prepared?
Passthrough AR can overlay automation on physical objects. Can yours?
Your desktop-only architecture may be incompatible with headset-based computing. Have you assessed this?
AR interfaces for consent approval could be more natural than desktop dialogs. Any plans?
Physical world automation (manufacturing, healthcare, logistics) via AR glasses is growing. Can yours reach it?
Spatial computing introduces new privacy risks for evidence chains. How does your model evolve?
Your local-first architecture might actually be an advantage in spatial computing. Is this intentional?
WebXR enables browser-based AR experiences. Could your browser automation work in WebXR?
Hands-free automation via voice and gaze is the primary input model for AR. Do you support it?
Contextual awareness (seeing what the user sees) could enhance your recipe automation. Planned?
Collaborative AR workflows will require multi-user evidence chains. Is your model designed for this?
Physical space as a trigger (enter meeting room → start recording) is an AR-native automation. Can yours?

Vision Pro's visionOS is a new platform that requires new app development. When do you ship for it?

questions: Apple Intelligence, Google Gemini, and Microsoft Copilot are becoming OS-native. What remains for you?
OS-native agents have permission to access any app, any file, any system resource. Your sandbox limits you.
Windows Recall will record and replay user activity natively. How does your evidence chain compete?
Android and iOS are shipping AI agents as built-in features. What is your response?
OS-level automation has lower latency, deeper integration, and better privacy than browser automation. Respond.
When iOS adds native web task delegation to Siri, your browser automation use case shrinks. Prepared?
systemd, launchd, and Task Scheduler already handle OS-level scheduling. How do you add value?
OS-native accessibility APIs (UIAutomation, Accessibility) are richer than browser DOM. Do you use them?
Desktop app automation (beyond browser) is a massive unserved market. Is your system limited to browser-only?
Your CLI is a good interface for OS automation. When do you expand beyond browser to desktop apps?
OS-level encryption (FileVault, BitLocker) already protects data. What does your vault add?
System notifications and focus modes are OS-native approval mechanisms. Do you integrate with them?
When every major OS ships an AI agent by default, what is your go-to-market strategy?
The OS vendor can revoke your browser automation permission at any time. How do you mitigate this risk?
Screen recording + AI (Apple Recall) makes your evidence chain redundant at the OS level. Respond.
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full domain. Prioritize questions that expose fixable competitive gaps.

questions: SHA-256 evidence chains will be vulnerable to quantum computers. What is your migration plan?
NIST has standardized post-quantum cryptography (CRYSTALS-Kyber, CRYSTALS-Dilithium). Are you planning migration?
Quantum computers will break RSA and ECC by 2030-2035. How does this affect your OAuth3 design?
Harvest now, decrypt later attacks are already being used on evidence data. Are your chains at risk?
Post-quantum TLS (TLS 1.3 with PQ extensions) is available in OpenSSL 3.4. Do you use it?
Your PBKDF2 key derivation uses SHA-256. What is your post-quantum key derivation strategy?
AES-256 is considered quantum-resistant in theory. Is your symmetric encryption adequate?
Your evidence chain's hash chain security depends on SHA-256. If broken, all historical evidence is invalid.
Zero-knowledge proofs could enhance your evidence chain without revealing content. Any plans?
The transition timeline for enterprises (5-10 years for full PQ migration) means starting now. Have you started?
Your OAuth3 spec will need post-quantum updates. Are you tracking IETF post-quantum OAuth work?
Lattice-based cryptography is the NIST-approved direction. How does this affect your implementation?
Post-quantum migration is a selling point for forward-looking enterprises. Is it in your roadmap?
Quantum random number generation could strengthen your cryptographic entropy. Any plans?
Your evidence chain integrity claim requires permanent cryptographic validity. How do you ensure this?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full domain. Prioritize questions that expose fixable competitive gaps.

questions: Show me the code. Not the readme — the actual implementation of the evidence chain.
Your PBKDF2 uses 100K iterations. NIST 2025 recommends 600K. Why the gap?
Your FSL license is interesting but converts to Apache 2.0 in 4 years. What prevents a fork before?
Local-first is a genuine differentiator. What does it mean in practice when the server is down?
Your hash chain uses SHA-256 without prev_hash. How is this a chain and not a collection?
Your OAuth3 spec — where is the formal specification and what makes it incompatible with OAuth2?
AES-256-GCM vault encryption with PBKDF2 — what is the salt strategy and rotation policy?
Your recipe format in YAML — what is the formal schema and how is it validated before execution?
BYOK with local Anthropic key — does the key ever leave the local machine? Show the code.
Your approval workflow — what prevents a timing attack between approval and execution?

Is there a public security advisory page for disclosed vulnerabilities?
Your daemon architecture — what prevents privilege escalation from the daemon process?
Show me the test coverage report. Not the number — the actual uncovered paths.
Your vault key is derived from machine_id. Any local process can decrypt all tokens. Is this fixed?

Could your product be used for targeted harassment or stalking at scale?
What external oversight body reviews your product for dual-use risk?
Has any military, intelligence, or defense organization approached you about your technology?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full domain. Prioritize questions that expose fixable competitive gaps.

questions: Where are we genuinely weaker? Plan to close gaps?
Name the 3 structural moats that competitors cannot copy without self-harm.
What is the switching cost for a user who has built 50 recipes and 6 months of evidence?
What are the 3 network effects that compound over time?
State your counter-position to each of the top 5 competitors in one sentence each.
Is the moat architectural (hard to copy) or execution-based (first-mover only)?
How long until the moat becomes defensible (18 months? 3 years?)
What is the honest list of current gaps that a well-funded competitor could exploit?
Which moat strengthens fastest with user growth?
Does the recipe flywheel actually produce network effects or is it linear growth?
What is the token cannibalization mechanism and why can't OpenAI implement it?
Is local-first a permanent moat or does cloud-native catch up on privacy?
What evidence chain accumulation creates the compounding switching cost?
Is the OAuth3 standard moat strengthened by adoption or weakened by imitation?
At what user count does the moat become genuinely defensible against a FAANG competitor?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Expand from seed questions to ~100 questions covering the full domain. Prioritize questions that expose fixable competitive gaps.

questions: Your best 3 engineers get poached. What IP walks out the door?
Google ships Chrome-native browser automation for free to 3 billion users. What do you do?
Anthropic revokes your API access with 30 days notice. What happens to your business?
Microsoft bundles a Copilot automation feature into M365 at no additional cost. Response?
A YC company clones your recipe format and raises $10M. How do you compete?
The EU bans AI agents that access personal data without explicit per-action consent. Impact?
By 2030, every major OS ships with a built-in AI agent. What remains for Solace?
Your sole founder gets hit by a bus. What is the continuity plan?
A security researcher publishes a critical vulnerability in your evidence chain. Response?
Your primary cloud provider raises prices 300%. What is your infrastructure migration plan?
A nation-state bans all AI automation tools. How does your local-first architecture help?
Your recipe library becomes the standard that competitors all support. What happens to your moat?
FDA mandates evidence chains for all clinical AI tools by 2027. Are you ready?
What is the world in 2030 where Solace won? What had to be true?
Compete on governance, not features. When every giant ships agents, who controls them safely?
